Great game. It's truly a shame to hear that the developer basically got bullied/harassed off the internet by a bunch of Bigots. 

Please remember to stand up against hate crimes, even if you're not the victim.
Even if the Dev stopped work on this game it still remains a really good game, i do hope eventually they start work on it again. Its one of those games thats cozy and fun would reccomend 10/10
I still really enjoy playing- just make sure you turn off chalk and mailbox in your settings so you don't get messed with by all the script kids.

the most relaxing chatroom game ever. fish and talk with people. too bad the lobbies are full of hackers exploiting the online functionality of this game that will never get patched because the dev was bullied and doxxed from the internet :steamsad:
Cowards bullied this wonderful creator away from a fantastic piece of art. Biggest tragedy in gaming for me. Awesome Fishing.

Great to run in the background while doing something else, especially when going for full journal completion by the time you've acquired all bait qualities and upgrades. Fun to chill with friends and chat too. Shame there will never be an update to address major issues, but at least I managed to finish my journal. Good times while they lasted.

yapping game
there's so many fishies to catch and it's supa fun to hang with friends and fish and ig u can also meet new people idk
but the fish are really cool and there's other stuff to do like gambling :3
A lofi fishing game for the fans of the old Animal Crossing on Gamecube. It's so chill and laid back, and it has some lovely little shenanigans to get up to baked into it.
very fun game with friends and also great game to chill out
Chill and laid back game to play in the background of something. Good for its price.
Best fishing game I've played and I play a lot of fishing games. Wish it would get more updates tho the potential here is crazy.
i like catching fish irl, and this pretty cool, f*** the scratch offs tho i cant hit a banger jackpot on them damn scratchies. but ya 10/10 tho, progression tuff lowkey

I found this to be quite a relaxing game. The colours just chill me out and allow me to fish in peace. You can play with others and 'meow' endlessly. You can also purchase items, draw, play music, gain cosmetics. There is surprisingly quite a bit to do in this little game as well as making new 'mysterious' discoveries~ e u e b I have not completed everything that there is to do here. Its a game to take at your own pace. You can customise and even gain new titles to display. Go find a friend or just sit by yourself and do a little fishing. Just have fun~

The game is fun, I have to admit. I've met some nice people, silly and quirky ones as well. There's bit of humor with the game too.

I loved playing the game while the fun lasted. Even if the dev is now absent due to stuff that happened to them, It was still a game where I can relax, talk or chat with good people and just go with the flow. With the lack of updates and security of the game, I guess it can get a little boring when you don't know what to do anymore. If you have friends or just wanna make friends if yer' lonely (like me)

Then this game is a good recommendation for anyone like that or who just wanna have fun.

got all fish, all achievements, very nice game, takes me back a bit to club penguin, lots of nice conversations, lots of friends to be made

TLDR: 
fih

Very cozy game. Has a lot of fantastic cosmetics for your character giving you a lot of freedom on how you customize yourself. Lobbies are still semi-active and are fun to hang out in. Would absolutely recommend if you are looking for a simple cozy game to hang out with friends or randoms.

This game is such a vibe for real, if you’re on the fence make sure to give the game a try. The only bad part is a good portion of the community isn’t as active, but this game has been a breath of fresh air.

Its grindy but fun cause you never know what you'll catch could be piece plastic or a fish that wont fit on a 80 inch tv the easter egg is really cool just wish after you'd collect all the stuff for the rod you didn't have to pay $500 (in game money) for it if you love fun grindy games 11/10 would recommend
Chill game. You can play the guitar, change your outfit, and play with other people. I love my pet turtle named Mikey in the game.
Cute game. Wish it had more players and was still being worked on. Definitely worth 5 dollars.

If you love Animal Crossing for only the fishing mechanics, then you will love the game! Amazing community, and a W game. It's so worth a try!

Want to play Animal crossing with essentially only the fishing?
Want a very casual laid back cozy game to sit around with some online friends and play?

This is your answer. Sit, fish, chat do a little wandering around, repeat.
I love this silly lil fishing game, it's great for just hanging out and talking to others while still being entertaining

Super simple game, its a chat room with fishing to put is bluntly. There is a few secrets to discover, upgrades to gain and fish to catch. Play solo, run a open or private game or join one of many servers. There's not a lot of fish but there is about 5 or 6 rarities of each fish you can catch so its not too bad. game runs smooth, sometimes there is lag in public servers but overall is a fairly solid game to relax too. you can even gamble haha
great to play with friends (or in my case) a partner :3 just a really chill game in general
its really cute and relaxing with a really great community attached to it
super fun and addicting very chill and laidback game 10/10 + amazing graphics

This game helped me get through hard times. It was a nice escape from everything going on in my life at the time. I got laid off and had to get a nightshift job. Me and my wife were on opposite schedules so I had to be quiet at night while she slept. This was a chill game to play that helped keep my mind off things bothering me. It's been awhile since I've played it, but I intend to play it again soon. It saddens me that this game will not get any more updates, but I'm curious about rift fishing. Hopefully it'll give the same vibe, but with new experiences. Thank you webfishing.

Great game would recommend 10/10 Very cool.

Love the customasibility and the fishing mechanic, Very cool

My only PROBLEM is that there is no blue lobster..

IN MY HUMBLE OPINION, i request there to be a halloween event where you have a chance to catch a blue lobster wich upon catching it, it will play the Toccota And Fugue in D Minor song PLEASE
Very chill and relaxing but also good humor and lots of things to work towards.
The atmosphere of this game lends itself to chill talks, gambling, and trying to get all the fish takes a WHILE. I like using my big closet to make cosplays!
Fun little chill game.  I just wish it supported controller and my old save file were still there.

A very chill and calming little fishing game. Even playing online with random people is pretty chill. Unlocking new gear and items is not overly grindy and a lot of the clothes/items are very silly and fun :3. I am sad that it won’t be receiving any more updates.

Only thing I think one should keep in mind is that this game has only local saves/has no cloud saves.

First of all, this game is beautiful, but DO NOT PLAY THIS GAME UNLESS YOU WANT VIRUSES

Everytime I see this games, I cry and die a little inside. You cannot play it safe anymore. Whether it was grinchers fault, the fault of the horrible, horrible ppl on twitter or the other hackers/nasty ppls, Idk who to blame.

For those who never got the chance to play, I feel very sorry for you. This game provided a sorta place where anybody from any corner of the world could sit down and talk to ppl, for hours, while keeping preoccuppied. This game helped me feel a sense of belonging and happiness when nobody else could, allowed me to make friends when I felt at one of the lower points of my life.

People often talk about spaces on the internet that were culled by the advertisers into becoming ikea showrooms, and this became the last sorta bastion I've seen in regards to that; a place untouched; no advertisers; no ulterior motives; you just got to be with ppl, got to make friends with ppl; got to experience happiness with ppl.

I feel like it represents the last point in my life where things felt simple, and happy, as if they ever were. And alike how my adolescence ended as my adulthood began, as did simplicity and wilfull ignorance, all the horrible people that spread hatred within our world came to haunt this world too.

I guess, in a way, its sort of a commentary on how some humans will rip innocence and love out of the dearest of things the collective of us like and 'colonise' it in a way, ripping something good from the hands of everybody. Oddly like how the world has been left stuffed up for future generations.

To those who come to read this steam page for the first time, I am truly sorry for the fact that the chance to experience such a beautiful thing was ripped away from you. I hope we can experience it again one day, and I hope this time all those hateful ppl that killed this game don't kill rift fishing when that comes out. I highly recommend trying that when it does though, hopefully it will hit similarly.

Adieu my friends, till we fish again

it's a good social game, the only issue in my honest opinion? idk, apparently the creator got the sprunki creator treatment and was chased off of the internet i think. If you like fishing with friends or solo, i'd say play this...

with strangers? probably not as often... (i'm trying really hard to remember why this game stopped being updated...)

I believe this was because I updated/reinstalled my widnows, but none of my other games had any issues or loss of progress.

NGL game is fun i had my good hours, some may say amazing hours, until i log in one frightful day and my account is poof gone.. all my fish, all my rods, my rank, everything, i hate you. never let me find you ever

webfishing is genuinely one of my favorite games of all time. it's dirt cheap, and it's such a charming, cute little experience. i haven't really 100%ed it yet and i don't know if i plan to, but i do know one thing.

i'm an artist. and the chalk mechanic is AMAZING.

the chalk drawing mechanic is so barebones, but i am so genuine when i say that webfishing is one of my favorite art programs. you get like 7 colors (red, yellow, green, blue, black, white, rainbow) and an eraser. there are 3 brush sizes that you can switch between by holding shift and control. that's it. that's all you get. but somehow, the brush feel with the chalk in this game is just *perfect*. i don't get this kind of feel with regular pixel art softwares or art softwares in general. i don't know how to describe it. it just works so well. i wish that art programs had the webfishing chalk.

you don't really get layers, and the few colors you get are all that's available, but something something limitation leads to innovation yada yada. this game has singlehandedly killed off my art block. every time i just can't bring myself to come up with something i just hop on a webfishing offline lobby and start chalking away. it's actually magical i love this game so much

i don't even really fish in this game anymore. i pretty much just play for the chalk mechanic.

but the gameplay is good too. really nice game for if you just want to feel busy while you're watching something in the background. i've had a really good time watching mlp and whatnot while just fishing away, trying to snag whatever quality of whatever fish i need to complete my collection.

tldr: cute little game that keeps you busy with virtual fish, also a top tier art software
This videogame mimics my visions in a weird, precise way. I don't like it. Play with caution, especially if you are a christian.

I love fish.

I don’t just love them in the normal sense. I don’t simply enjoy eating them or watching them swim around in some overpriced aquarium at a dentist’s office. No. My love for fish has transcended the boundaries of common sense and entered a realm of obsession that scholars would spend decades studying, only to throw their notes into the ocean because they realized they would never truly understand the depth no, the abyssal trench of my affection for fish.

Fish are not just animals.
Fish are not just food.

Fish are a cosmic gift.

When I think about fish, my mind ignites like a deep sea vent spewing liquid fire beneath the crushing weight of 16,000 PSI. The mere idea of fish sends ripples through my soul. The word “fish” alone could make me drop to my knees as if Poseidon himself has descended from the briny depths and slapped me across the face with a halibut.

But let me explain properly, thoroughly, and with more intensity than anyone has ever requested.

Fish as Food: A Culinary Opera

To say fish taste good is like saying the ocean is “wet.”
It is an understatement so criminal it should be prosecuted in maritime court.

Fish can be:

grilled
fried
baked
steamed
smoked
cured
dried
stewed
seared
skewered
salted
fermented
marinated
sliced thin like edible rose petals
or eaten raw, shimmering like ocean gemstones

A grilled salmon? Sublime.
A fried catfish? Transcendent.
A raw tuna sashimi? A religious experience.

Every type of fish is its own symphony. Salmon is a buttery violin solo, tuna is a power chord, cod is the gentle hum of a choir, and sardines are a surprise kazoo section that somehow works.

And the flakes, my god, the flakes.
When a perfectly cooked piece of fish separates under the slightest touch of a fork?
That’s not food.
That’s biology creating art.

Fish as History: The Original Lifeline

Humanity would not exist without fish.

Entire civilizations were born because someone looked at a river and said, “Hey, what if I grab one of those shiny things?”

Fish fueled trade, culture, survival, and mythology.
They’re on ancient murals.
They’re in legends.
They’re in folk medicine.
They’re in religious texts.
They’re probably in your freezer right now.

Fishing is one of the oldest human activities. Before grocery stores existed, before food delivery apps existed, before the concept of a “diet” was invented there were fish, and people catching fish, and people eating fish.

Fish as Creatures: Liquid Grace

Watching a fish swim is like watching poetry written in water.

The gentle sway of a pectoral fin…
The smooth curl of the body as it turns…
The way light dances on scales like glittering armor…

Fish move with a serenity that puts humans to shame.

Fish live in an alien world, weightless and fluid, slipping between currents with the effortlessness of a dream. Some glow. Some fly (kind of). Some literally inflate into spheres when threatened because fish are innovators.

Fish are the world’s weirdest, most fascinating creatures. And I say that with admiration, not judgment.

Fish in My Thoughts: Permanent Residents

I think about fish constantly.

When I wake up? Fish.
When I eat breakfast? Fish.
When someone asks me a question completely unrelated to fish? I still find a way to think about fish.

I think about salmon migrating upstream with the determination of someone trying to get home before their food delivery is left at the door.
I think about lanternfish glowing in the endless dark like tiny aquatic night lights.
I think about groupers, flounders, sturgeons, barracudas, the whole cast of nature’s underwater RPG party.

Sometimes I lie awake at night wondering what a fish dreams about.
Do they dream of bigger oceans?
Do they dream of becoming sushi?

I must know.

Fish Are Everything

Fish are beauty.
Fish are food.
Fish are culture.
Fish are mystery.
Fish are science.
Fish are poetry.
Fish are the heartbeat of oceans, rivers, lakes, and sushi restaurants worldwide.

I do not merely admire fish.
I do not simply enjoy them.

I revere them.

Fish occupy mental real estate that I never even offered them. 

Because fish in all their forms, from the tiniest guppy to the thiccest tuna are nothing short of extraordinary.

WEBFISHING Steam Review Draft 🎣

WEBFISHING is less of a fishing game and more of a cozy little online porch where you and the homies can decompress.

Recommended if …
You want something chill to play with friends while catching up after a long week.

Not recommended if …
You need intense progression, challenge, or deep mechanical complexity.

What it is:
A relaxed multiplayer fishing game where the real point is hanging out, chatting, catching fish, and enjoying the little surprises tucked around the world.

What it nails:

Perfect mindless cozy gameplay when you want to unwind.
Great as a “catch up with the homies” game.
Has enough charm and little easter eggs to make the world feel playful.
Low-pressure in the best way. You can just exist, fish, talk, and vibe.

Where it slips:

Honestly, this is not the kind of game where I’m looking for flaws. Its simplicity is kind of the point.

Final call:
Absolutely loved it. WEBFISHING is the kind of game you throw on after a long week when everybody’s tired but still wants to be together. Cozy, funny, relaxed, and exactly what it’s trying to be.
honestly this game is great. its relaxing honestly. still has a community of people playing and everyone is friendly. fun game all around.
